title: Fable 5 Just Got Banned, Here’s What To Do
summary: A video argues that model bans don't eliminate capability, they redirect it elsewhere. The creator suggests frontier model restrictions are temporary ecosystem friction and that alternatives like Opus 4.8 remain available and competitive.
writeup: Jack Roberts responds to a ban on Fable 5 by arguing that restricting a frontier model does not remove the underlying capability, it shifts demand toward other systems. The framing treats such bans as temporary ecosystem friction rather than a hard ceiling on what users can do. The concrete takeaway: alternatives such as Opus 4.8 remain available and competitive, so the practical path is to move to those models.
